message:
fix(config): reject non-finite extension risk alpha values and add proptest coverage

Add sanitize_alpha() helper that filters out NaN, Infinity, and
-Infinity before clamping the alpha parameter to [1e-6, 0.5]. Previously
non-finite alpha values from config or environment variables would
propagate through and produce nonsensical risk calculations.

The sanitization is applied in both the config-file path
(cfg.alpha.and_then(sanitize_alpha)) and the environment variable path
(parse_env_f64(...).and_then(sanitize_alpha)), ensuring consistent
validation regardless of input source.

Add proptest coverage (128 cases each) verifying:
- Finite alpha values are clamped to [1e-6, 0.5]
- Non-finite values (NaN, Inf, -Inf) fall back to defaults
- Config deserialization roundtrips preserve alpha values
- Boundary values at the clamping edges produce expected results
- Combined alpha + window_size + min_samples config interaction
- Environment variable alpha parsing respects the same sanitization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
